headline: Domestic Violence Shelters at Capacity With 1,400 Women Turned Away
context: Government and NGO shelters for domestic violence survivors reached maximum capacity turning away 1,400 women in 2025.

cards.big: 1,400 women fleeing domestic violence turned away from full shelters in 2025
cards.sub: Malaysia has 52 shelters with total capacity of 1,800 beds for 33 million population

cards.big: Domestic violence police reports increased 35% from 2022 to 2025 reaching 9,800 cases
cards.sub: Rising reports reflect both increased violence and improved reporting willingness

cards.big: Domestic Violence Act 1994 provides protection orders but average issuance takes 14 days
cards.sub: 2-week delay leaves survivors in danger during the most critical escape period

cards.big: Spain operates 1 shelter per 10,000 women with 24-hour emergency placement guarantee
cards.sub: Statutory obligation to shelter ensures no survivor is turned away regardless of capacity

cards.big: Every woman turned away from a full shelter is sent back to the danger she tried to escape
Should Malaysia guarantee emergency shelter access for all domestic violence survivors within 24 hours
